Title: DcMYB113, a root-specific R2R3-MYB, conditions anthocyanin biosynthesis and modification in carrot

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Purple haze; Cosmic purple; Kurodagosun; Deep purple
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root periderm, phloem, and in some genotypes petiole
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with anthocyanin accumulation and acts as DcMYB113 partner in anthocyanin induction
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High transcript levels in purple root tissues of Purple haze, Deep purple and Cosmic purple; undetectable in nonpurple root tissues of Purple haze, Cosmic purple and Kurodagosun
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in 35S:DcMYB113 transgenic Kurodagosun roots
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression association; yeast two-hybrid interaction with DcMYB113
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential partner gene for stacking with DcMYB113 to enhance anthocyanin production
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DcbHLH3 expression correlates with purple pigmentation in carrot root tissues. 2. DcMYB113 physically interacts with DcbHLH3 in yeast. 3. DcbHLH3 transcript abundance increases in DcMYB113-overexpressing lines, supporting a cooperative role in anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although DcbHLH3 is a transcription factor, in this paper it is mainly treated as a DcMYB113-interacting downstream partner rather than the primary experimental subject.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose from UDP-xylose to cyanidin-3-galactoside to form cyanidin 3-xylosylgalactoside
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product and forms intermediate toward Cy3XSGG
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key anthocyanin modification step; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stable purple pigmentation and improved anthocyanin stability
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Carrot root tissues, especially purple tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in purple root tissues of Purple haze, Deep purple and Cosmic purple; virtually undetectable in nonpurple tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Sharply increased in 35S:DcMYB113 and ProPPHZ:DcMYB113 transgenic Kurodagosun lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein enzyme assay in E. coli followed by HPLC-MS; qRT-PCR; promoter binding by yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase transactivation by DcMYB113
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering more stable, highly glycosylated anthocyanin profiles when stacked with DcMYB113
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin glycosylation by converting Cy3G to Cy3XG, contributing to accumulation of modified stable anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant DcUCGXT1 converts cyanidin-3-O-galactoside to Cy3XG using UDP-xylose. 2. DcUCGXT1 expression associates with purple pigmentation. 3. DcMYB113 binds and activates the DcUCGXT1 promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified within a previously proposed anthocyanin composition QTL (Q3).

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes addition of sinapoylglucose-derived acyl group to anthocyanins, enhancing Cy3XSGG formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for modified terminal anthocyanin formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key anthocyanin acylation step; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stable purple pigmentation and enhanced anthocyanin stability
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Carrot root tissues, especially purple tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in purple root tissues of Purple haze, Deep purple and Cosmic purple; virtually undetectable in nonpurple tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Sharply increased in 35S:DcMYB113 and ProPPHZ:DcMYB113 transgenic Kurodagosun lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S overexpression in Deep purple calli followed by HPLC-MS; qRT-PCR; promoter binding by yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase transactivation by DcMYB113
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for tailoring acylated anthocyanin composition and pigment stability in carrot improvement
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances production of acylated anthocyanin Cy3XSGG in carrot
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DcSAT1 overexpression in purple carrot calli causes Cy3XSGG to become the main anthocyanin. 2. DcSAT1 expression correlates with purple tissues. 3. DcMYB113 binds and activates the DcSAT1 promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified within a previously proposed anthocyanin composition QTL (Q2); no corresponding predicted gene was present in the carrot genome annotation used for WGCNA.

Regulation_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: gDNA: MK896874; cDNA: MK896875; promoter: MN149519; genomic locus candidate DCAR_008994
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB family; clusters with AtMYB113 anthocyanin regulators
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and downstream glycosylation/acylation; likely anthocyanin transport regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DcbHLH3; DcCHS1; DcCHI1; DcF3H1; DcF3'H1; DcDFR1; DcLDOX1; DcUCGalT1; DcUCGXT1; DcSAT1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of DcbHLH3 and tested anthocyanin structural/modification genes; directly binds promoters of DcUCGXT1 and DcSAT1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Root-specific decisive activator controlling anthocyanin accumulation and modification pattern in Purple haze carrot
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms/likely functions in MBW-like complex through interaction with DcbHLH3
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Promoter variation determines root-specific expression; an additional unknown genetic factor may affect xylem expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: anthocyanin, especially cyanidin-based and modified anthocyanins such as Cy3XSGG
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root periderm and phloem in Purple haze; root and petiole when driven by 35S; root in ProPPHZ transgenics
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in Purple haze root periderm and phloem; undetectable in Purple haze xylem and in roots of Deep purple, Cosmic purple and Kurodagosun
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in transgenic Kurodagosun roots; 35S drives root and petiole pigmentation, ProPPHZ drives root-preferential expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq and qRT-PCR; transgenic overexpression in Cosmic purple and Kurodagosun; promoter complementation with ProPPHZ:DcMYB113; yeast two-hybrid; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase reporter assays; metabolite profiling; WGCNA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induces anthocyanin biosynthesis in carrot and confers root-specific pigmentation when driven by its native Purple haze promoter
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter; native Purple haze DcMYB113 promoter (root-specific/preferential)
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq root tissues: PRJNA540112/SRP194046; transgenic root tissues: PRJNA548023/SRP200826; de novo assembly raw reads: SRR2146943
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering purple carrot roots and customized tissue specificity; stacking with modification genes could optimize stable anthocyanin composition
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: 35S-driven expression causes ectopic purple petiole and broader root pigmentation than native pattern
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DcMYB113 was identified within the P1 locus as the candidate gene for root-specific anthocyanin pigmentation in carrot. 2. Its expression is specific to Purple haze purple root tissues and appears controlled by promoter variation. 3. Overexpression or complementation with DcMYB113 induces anthocyanin accumulation in anthocyanin-deficient backgrounds. 4. DcMYB113 activates DcbHLH3, anthocyanin biosynthetic genes, and the modification genes DcUCGXT1 and DcSAT1. 5. Coexpression analysis suggests it may also influence anthocyanin transport.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DcMYB113 from Purple haze complements CPP and Kurodagosun, but transgenic Kurodagosun showed purple xylem unlike Purple haze, implying another unknown factor modulates xylem-specific expression/accumulation.
